channel.title: Teaching on Michael Healy's ePortfolio
channel.description: Recent content in Teaching on Michael Healy's ePortfolio

item.title: Teaching Philosophy
item.description: Old - To Update I view writing as the extension of thought into a physical form. Writing is not only able to convey information it also creates and changes meaning. Writing is in dialogue with the writer, the audience, and the broader context of culture and society. I want students to be able to enter into this dialogue as full participants, not just people who just consume and regurgitate the thoughts of others.

item.title: ENC 3021 - Rhetoric
item.description: Selection of feedback and materials from my teaching of ENC 3021: Rhetoric. Includes course sites, evaluation materials, lessons examples, and course materials.

item.title: ENC 1101 - Freshman Composition and Rhetoric
item.description: Materials and feedback from my teaching of ENC 1101. Includes course sites, evaluation materials, lessons examples, and course materials.

item.title: College Composition @ FSU
item.description: Have taught within the college composition program at UNO and FSU. Taught English Composition I and Composition II at UNO. Have taught ENC 1101 and ENC 2135 at FSU. My pedagogy focuses on exploration, observation, play, and collaboration inside and outside the class.

item.title: ENC 2135 - Genres, Research, and Context
item.description: Materials and feedback from my teaching of ENC 2135. Includes course sites, evaluation materials, lessons examples, and course materials.

item.title: Writing Center University of Nebraska Omaha
